title: Jones stronger AlgEquiv product theorem deferred
summary: The current Jones finite-dimensional packet does not prove the stronger subalgebra-level AlgEquiv product theorem and should not be retrieved as though that stronger claim were already closed.
assumptions: The current bounded route intentionally stops short of the stronger algebra-level product equivalence.

boundary_conditions: the current route is theorem-packet local
exclusions: claiming the stronger subalgebra-level AlgEquiv product theorem

applies_to: the current Jones finite-product packet and its reusable route capsule
out_of_scope: future work that explicitly closes the stronger algebra-level product theorem

citations: formal_theory_review comparator risks for the Jones Chapter 4 packet

promotion.rationale: Promote the strongest currently hidden Jones limitation into a dedicated warning note so retrieval can answer limitation questions without over-reading theorem prose.
